### Spec
#### Schema
```yaml
autoscalingPolicy:
  maxNodes: integer
  minNodes: integer
  mode: string
description: string
initialSize: integer
maintenancePolicy: string
maintenanceWindow:
  startTime: string
nodeTemplateRef:
  external: string
  name: string
  namespace: string
resourceID: string
shareSettings:
  projectMap:
  - idRef:
      external: string
      name: string
      namespace: string
    projectIdRef:
      external: string
      name: string
      namespace: string
  shareType: string
size: integer
zone: string
```

<tbody>
    <tr>
        <td>
            <p><code>autoscalingPolicy</code></p>
            <p><i>Optional</i></p>
        </td>
        <td>
            <p><code class="apitype">object</code></p>
            <p>Immutable. If you use sole-tenant nodes for your workloads, you can use the node group autoscaler to automatically manage the sizes of your node groups.</p>
        </td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
        <td>
            <p><code>autoscalingPolicy.maxNodes</code></p>
            <p><i>Optional</i></p>
        </td>
        <td>
            <p><code class="apitype">integer</code></p>
            <p>Immutable. Maximum size of the node group. Set to a value less than or equal to 100 and greater than or equal to min-nodes.</p>
        </td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
        <td>
            <p><code>autoscalingPolicy.minNodes</code></p>
            <p><i>Optional</i></p>
        </td>
        <td>
            <p><code class="apitype">integer</code></p>
            <p>Immutable. Minimum size of the node group. Must be less than or equal to max-nodes. The default value is 0.</p>
        </td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
        <td>
            <p><code>autoscalingPolicy.mode</code></p>
            <p><i>Optional</i></p>
        </td>
        <td>
            <p><code class="apitype">string</code></p>
            <p>Immutable. The autoscaling mode. Set to one of the following: - OFF: Disables the autoscaler. - ON: Enables scaling in and scaling out. - ONLY_SCALE_OUT: Enables only scaling out. You must use this mode if your node groups are configured to restart their hosted VMs on minimal servers. Possible values: ["OFF", "ON", "ONLY_SCALE_OUT"].</p>
        </td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
        <td>
            <p><code>description</code></p>
            <p><i>Optional</i></p>
        </td>
        <td>
            <p><code class="apitype">string</code></p>
            <p>Immutable. An optional textual description of the resource.</p>
        </td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
        <td>
            <p><code>initialSize</code></p>
            <p><i>Optional</i></p>
        </td>
        <td>
            <p><code class="apitype">integer</code></p>
            <p>Immutable. The initial number of nodes in the node group. One of 'initial_size' or 'size' must be specified.</p>
        </td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
        <td>
            <p><code>maintenancePolicy</code></p>
            <p><i>Optional</i></p>
        </td>
        <td>
            <p><code class="apitype">string</code></p>
            <p>Immutable. Specifies how to handle instances when a node in the group undergoes maintenance. Set to one of: DEFAULT, RESTART_IN_PLACE, or MIGRATE_WITHIN_NODE_GROUP. The default value is DEFAULT.</p>
        </td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
        <td>
            <p><code>maintenanceWindow</code></p>
            <p><i>Optional</i></p>
        </td>

    </tr>
    <tr>
        <td>
            <p><code>maintenanceWindow.startTime</code></p>
            <p><i>Required*</i></p>
        </td>
        <td>
            <p><code class="apitype">string</code></p>
            <p>Immutable. instances.start time of the window. This must be in UTC format that resolves to one of 00:00, 04:00, 08:00, 12:00, 16:00, or 20:00. For example, both 13:00-5 and 08:00 are valid.</p>
        </td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
        <td>

### Status
#### Schema
```yaml
conditions:
- lastTransitionTime: string
  message: string
  reason: string
  status: string
  type: string
creationTimestamp: string
observedGeneration: integer
selfLink: string
```

        <td>

## Sample YAML(s)

### Typical Use Case
```yaml
apiVersion: compute.cnrm.cloud.google.com/v1beta1
kind: ComputeNodeGroup
metadata:
  name: computenodegroup-sample
spec:
  description: A single sole-tenant node in the us-central1-b zone.
  size: 1
  nodeTemplateRef:
    name: computenodegroup-dep
  zone: us-central1-b
---
apiVersion: compute.cnrm.cloud.google.com/v1beta1
kind: ComputeNodeTemplate
metadata:
  name: computenodegroup-dep
spec:
  region: us-central1
  nodeType: n1-node-96-624
```

Note: If you have any trouble with instantiating the resource, refer to <a href="https://docs.cloud.google.com/config-connector/docs/troubleshooting">Troubleshoot Config Connector</a>.
